input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version:** Install the latest stable release. MAUI release notes specify the minimum supported Xcode version — check [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases).
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **.NET 8 or .NET 9 SDK** (whichever the current MAUI stable release targets).
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI NuGet page ([nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk)) lists the required .NET SDK version in its dependencies.
- **Install:** Download the macOS (Arm64 or x64) installer.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- **Install:**
  ```bash
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"
  dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui  # should scaffold a project
  ```

## 4. Android SDK & JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically. To manage them explicitly:

- **Android Command-Line Tools:** Bundled via the workload, or install from [developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only).
- **JDK:** The MAUI workload ships the **Microsoft OpenJDK** build. If you need it standalone: [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download).
- **Android Emulator (optional):** Install Android Studio from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) for the emulator and device manager UI.
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android   # builds for Android
  ```
- **Version discovery:** Run `dotnet workload search maui` and check the [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) for required Android API levels.

## 5. iOS Simulators

```bash
xcodebuild -downloadPlatform iOS
xcrun simctl list devices available
```

## 6. Full Verification (end-to-end)

```bash
# Create and build for both platforms
dotnet new maui -n VerifySetup
cd VerifySetup

# iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
# Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Recommended IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + `.NET MAUI` extension |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (native MAUI support) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been **retired** — VS Code with the MAUI extension or Rider are the current recommended options on macOS.

## Quick-reference install order

```
1. Xcode              (from App Store)
2. xcode-select       (point to Xcode)
3. .NET SDK           (from dot.net)
4. dotnet workload install maui
5. (Optional) Android Studio for emulator
6. VS Code + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension
```
